summary: The United States conducted self‑defence strikes on missile sites and Iranian boats in southern Iran. Israel intensified operations in southern Lebanon, targeting over 100 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and ordering the evacuation of Nabatieh residents. Iran publicly demanded $24 billion in frozen assets as part of any U.S. deal and reported the death of four regime forces on Larak Island.
key_points: U.S. forces carried out self‑defence strikes in southern Iran against missile launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to lay mines (Source: @shin_persian/6234).
Israel’s air force attacked more than 100 terror infrastructure targets in South Lebanon, including weapons depots and command posts (Source: @inon_yttach/8307).
The IDF issued an evacuation order for residents of Nabatieh, directing them north of the Zahrani River due to Hezbollah ceasefire violations (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3044).
Iran demanded the release of $24 billion in frozen funds as a condition of any U.S. agreement, with half to be released immediately (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3043).
President Trump announced that enriched uranium would be transferred to the United States or destroyed in coordination with Iran under international supervision (Source: @shin_persian/6231).
An Israeli senior official stated Israel will complete its mission in Iran, noting political considerations about U.S. participation (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/361542).
Four regime forces were reported killed on Larak Island in Hormozgan Province following a strike, according to Iranian news agencies (Source: @shin_persian/6228).

sections.executive_summary: U.S. self‑defence strikes hit Iranian missile sites and boats in the south. Israel expanded its campaign in southern Lebanon, striking over 100 Hezbollah targets and ordering Nabatieh’s evacuation. Iran’s demand for $24 billion frozen assets and reports of casualties on Larak Island heighten regional tensions.

points: Iran publicly demanded $24 billion in frozen assets be released as part of any U.S. deal, with half to be made available immediately upon announcement (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3043).
Iranian media reported that four regime forces were killed on Larak Island in Hormozgan Province after a strike on Monday, 4th of Khordad (Source: @shin_persian/6228).

U.S. Central Command spokesperson Capt. Tim Hawkins confirmed U.S. self‑defence strikes in southern Iran targeting missile launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to lay mines (Source: @shin_persian/6234).

President Trump stated that enriched uranium would be transferred to the United States or destroyed in coordination with Iran, with the Atomic Energy Commission witnessing the process (Source: @shin_persian/6231).
The Israeli Air Force attacked more than 100 terror infrastructure sites in South Lebanon, including weapons depots, command centers, and observation posts (Source: @inon_yttach/8307).

The IDF ordered an immediate evacuation of residents from the city of Nabatieh, directing them to move north of the Zahrani River due to Hezbollah ceasefire violations (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3044).
A senior Israeli official indicated Israel will complete its mission in Iran, noting that political considerations involve the United States not joining (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/361542).
Hezbollah launched multiple explosive drones that entered Israeli territory, some intercepted and some landing without casualties, prompting alarms in Netanya (Source: @inon_yttach/8320).
Israeli air operations in South Lebanon targeted over 90 weapons warehouses, command posts, and observation points belonging to Hezbollah (Source: @inon_yttach/8307).
The IDF’s evacuation warning for Nabatieh cited ongoing Hezbollah ceasefire violations and risk to civilians near Hezbollah sites (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3044).
Hezbollah’s drone attacks caused false alarms in Netanya and an alarm in the Netanya‑Al‑Aramsha area, though no injuries were reported (Source: @inon_yttach/8320).
Israeli security command announced upcoming pre‑emptive guidance for rocket and missile fire from Lebanon when operational conditions allow (Source: @New_security8200/199932).
U.S. forces conducted self‑defence strikes in southern Iran against missile sites and Iranian boats, as part of protecting U.S. troops in the region (Source: @shin_persian/6234).
Iranian reports indicated explosions near Bandar Abbas airport and in the Persian Gulf, with multiple loud blasts heard near the airport and offshore vessels (Source: @VahidOnline/75714).

key_developments: U.S. forces carried out self‑defence strikes in southern Iran against missile launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to lay mines (Source: @shin_persian/6234).
Israel’s air force attacked more than 100 terror infrastructure targets in South Lebanon, including weapons depots and command posts (Source: @inon_yttach/8307).
The IDF issued an evacuation order for residents of Nabatieh, directing them north of the Zahrani River due to Hezbollah ceasefire violations (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3044).
Iran demanded the release of $24 billion in frozen funds as part of any U.S. agreement, with half to be released immediately upon announcement (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3043).
President Trump announced that enriched uranium would be transferred to the United States or destroyed in coordination with Iran under international supervision (Source: @shin_persian/6231).
An Israeli senior official stated Israel will complete its mission in Iran, noting political considerations about U.S. participation (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/361542).
Four regime forces were reported killed on Larak Island in Hormozgan Province following a strike, according to Iranian news agencies (Source: @shin_persian/6228).
sections.threat_assessment: The region faces heightened risk of direct confrontation as the United States escalates military actions against Iranian assets, while Israel intensifies strikes in Lebanon and signals broader objectives in Iran. Iranian demands for frozen assets and statements on enriched uranium add diplomatic pressure, potentially prompting retaliatory measures. Hezbollah’s drone incursions and Israeli evacuation orders further destabilize the Lebanon front, increasing the likelihood of cross‑border clashes.
sections.regional_response: U.S. officials emphasize restraint while defending forces, yet continue offensive operations in southern Iran. Israeli leadership coordinates with domestic security agencies to adjust civil defense protocols and expand operational reach in Lebanon. Iran leverages diplomatic channels, demanding asset releases and negotiating nuclear material disposition, while maintaining a narrative of defending sovereignty.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of U.S. strike locations and Iranian naval movements in the Persian Gulf is critical for anticipating further engagements. Israeli targeting patterns in southern Lebanon suggest a focus on degrading Hezbollah logistics ahead of potential ground operations. Iranian internal reports of casualties on Larak Island may indicate covert strike capabilities affecting regional security calculations.
